name: Cron Job Management
description: Schedule, manage, and debug recurring tasks using cron and systemd timers for automated system maintenance.

steps.title: List current user's cron jobs
steps.description: View all scheduled tasks for the current user.

steps.title: Edit the crontab
steps.description: Open the crontab editor to add or modify scheduled tasks.
steps.tip: Cron format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week command. Use crontab.guru to verify expressions.

steps.title: View system-wide cron jobs
steps.description: Check for cron jobs defined in system directories.

steps.title: List systemd timers (modern alternative)
steps.description: View all scheduled timers managed by systemd.
steps.tip: Systemd timers are more flexible than cron and can depend on other services, handle missed runs, and provide better logging.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Cron Job Management
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== User Crontab ==="
crontab -l 2>/dev/null || echo "No crontab for $(whoami)"

echo ""
echo "=== System Cron Jobs ==="
for dir in /etc/cron.d /etc/cron.daily /etc/cron.hourly /etc/cron.weekly /etc/cron.monthly; do
  if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
    echo "--- $dir ---"
    ls "$dir" 2>/dev/null || echo "Empty"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== Systemd Timers ==="
systemctl list-timers --no-pager 2>/dev/null | head -15

echo ""
echo "=== Cron Syntax Reference ==="
echo "* * * * *  command"
echo "| | | | |"
echo "| | | | +-- Day of week (0-7, Sun=0 or 7)"
echo "| | | +---- Month (1-12)"
echo "| | +------ Day of month (1-31)"
echo "| +-------- Hour (0-23)"
echo "+---------- Minute (0-59)"
echo ""
echo "Examples:"
echo "  0 2 * * *   Daily at 2:00 AM"
echo "  */15 * * * * Every 15 minutes"
echo "  0 9 * * 1-5  Weekdays at 9:00 AM"

faq.question: How do I debug a cron job that is not running?
faq.answer: Check the cron log: 'grep CRON /var/log/syslog' or 'journalctl -u cron'. Common issues: wrong PATH (use full paths), permission errors, or the cron daemon not running.

faq.question: How do I capture cron job output?
faq.answer: Redirect stdout and stderr in the crontab: '0 2 * * * /path/to/script.sh >> /var/log/myjob.log 2>&1'. To suppress all output: '0 2 * * * /path/to/script.sh > /dev/null 2>&1'.
